45.reason: [Using 'delete' as variable name is invalid in C++ but allowed in C; however, it's a reserved word in C++ and may cause issues with some compilers or coding standards. But more critically, the real issue is that the traversal functions assume graph remains connected and always start from 0, but the current DFS/BFS implementations loop over all vertices and start DFS/BFS from every unvisited vertex, violating requirement #3 which says traversal must start from vertex 0 only.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [Using 'delete' as variable name is invalid in C++ but allowed in C; however, it's a reserved word in C++ and may cause issues with some compilers or coding standards. But more critically, the real issue is that the traversal functions assume graph remains connected and always start from 0, but the current DFS/BFS implementations loop over all vertices and start DFS/BFS from every unvisited vertex, violating requirement #3 which says traversal must start from vertex 0 only.]

128.reason: [This loop starts DFS from every unvisited vertex, but problem requires traversal to always start from vertex 0 only. This violates requirement #3. Should only call DFS(0) if 0 is not deleted.]

183.reason: [Redundant assignment: visit[start] is set to 1 twice. Not logically wrong but unnecessary. However, the bigger issue is same as DFS: BFS is called for every unvisited component, but should only start from 0.]
